text:
Bear with me but I dug deep into this. 

Credo is sampling the Cardinal DSP family to lead customers. 

1.6T transceiver was in demonstration-  stage at OFC but based on bluebird dsp

Bluebird was first gen dsp while this new cardinal dsp is second gen and was engineered with integrated EML + silicon photonics drivers, which is what ties it to the DustPhotonics acquisition.

Owning the DSP, the silicon photonics, and the transceiver assembly is vertical integration

optical transceiver manufacturers buy DSP silicon and build it into modules. The major names: Fabrinet, Coherent, Lumentum, Innolight, Eoptolink, plus others. DSP is a key component inside. 

But when Credo sells its own ZeroFlap transceiver, it competes with these module makers instead — the vertical-integration angle from earlier.)

The end demand. Google and Amazon are qualifying 1.6T ￼, with the broader set being Microsoft, Meta, Oracle, and the neoclouds. They don’t usually buy the DSP directly, but they run the qualification that determines which DSP wins. 

However Once a module maker qualifies a Marvell or Broadcom DSP into a platform that a hyperscaler has blessed, switching to Credo means re-qualifying and that’s not as simple. 

So that’s where the vertically integrated nature is a game changer. 

By owning the DSP (Cardinal/Bluebird), the silicon photonics/laser engine (DustPhotonics), and the transceiver assembly with reliability telemetry (ZeroFlap/PILOT), 
$CRDO controls the three highest-value blocks of the 1.6T module in-house — capturing more BOM dollars per link, tuning the electrical-optical-DSP interfaces together for a lower-power and more reliable module than rivals stitching together third-party components, and sidestepping the merchant-DSP socket fight by selling a differentiated whole module rather than a chip that has to displace entrenched Marvell/Broadcom silicon.

Their is why their guidance increase was highly encouraging. They are winning in optical and likely entire modules 3 digit ASPs.

text:
People think the AI infrastructure / photonics / semiconductor trade is over because some stocks already ran.

Think again.

The market is still massively underestimating what Europe is about to do through the EU Chips Act and now Chips Act 2.0.

The EU is actively pushing to accelerate domestic semiconductor demand, AI infrastructure, and photonic chip development.

Years from now, this decade may not be remembered for apps or software…

but for the Physical AI infrastructure layer that quietly rebuilt the world underneath it.

text:
China is blessed: 
Silicon Photonics and Chip-level Optical I/O. At a major IEEE conference, Huawei formally introduced 'Tao's Law' (\tau-Law), which highlights their massive breakthrough in replacing traditional copper wires with lasers and fiber optics right at the chip level

$IQE.L 업데이트

IQE가 6월 3일 RNS를 통해 Artisan Partners 계열의 major holding 변동을 공시함.

핵심은 Artisan Partners 측 IQE 의결권 지분이 기존 10.60%에서 13.10%로 올라갔다는 점임.

공시 기준으로 Artisan 측은 IQE 보통주 의결권 171,991,595개를 보유하고 있고, 이는 전체 의결권의 13.10%임.

금융상품을 통한 추가 의결권은 0%.

기준일은 2026년 6월 1일, 회사 통보일은 6월 3일임.

다만 이걸 “Artisan이 장내에서 대규모로 추가 매수했다”고 바로 단정하면 안 됨.

이번 TR-1에는 voting rights 취득/처분뿐 아니라, 총 의결권 구조 변화도 같이 반영된 것으로 보임.

IQE는 최근 fundraising 완료 이후 발행주식수와 총 의결권 기준이 바뀌었음.

그래서 실제 추가 매수인지, 배정 참여인지, 의결권 기준 변화 영향인지까지는 추가 확인이 필요함.

그래도 긍정적으로 볼 부분은 있음.

최근 IQE는 £81M 자금조달을 마쳤고, MACOM 전략투자와 장기공급계약도 들어왔음.

여기에 Artisan 같은 기관 주주가 10%대에서 13%대로 올라온 공시가 나오면서, IQE의 주주구성과 자본구조 재편이 본격적으로 드러나고 있음.

물론 이건 신규 고객계약도 아니고, InP / AI datacenter photonics 매출 전환 확인도 아님.

이번 Artisan 지분 공시는 강한 펀더멘털 호재라기보다는 hold-strengthening 보조 신호에 가까움.

단순 테마성 반등이 아니라, 전략투자자와 기관 주주가 동시에 의미 있는 지분을 보유하는 구조로 바뀌고 있다는 점은 긍정적임.

다만 dilution 이후 주주구성 재편 과정이기 때문에 과도한 해석은 금물임.

다음 체크포인트는 세 가지임.

Artisan 지분 증가의 정확한 원인
MACOM 측 이사 선임 완료 여부
MACOM LTSA와 AI photonics 수요가 실제 revenue / EBITDA recovery로 연결되는지

개인 기록 / 투자 조언 아님.

text:
https://t.co/7ALZf7Eb0a

Squeezed states of light represent one of the most fundamental concepts  in quantum optics. They play a crucial role in various fields of  science, quantum technology, and photonics, with applications in quantum  information processing, quantum computation, quantum sensing, and  gravitational wave detection. In this paper, we propose a plasmonic  waveguide for the generation of squeezed states via four-wave mixing.

text:
AI半導体投資日報_260608_朝刊

■朝刊の見取り図
・対象期間は、2026年6月7日夕刊後から6月8日朝まで。
・前回夕刊では、電力、冷却、HBM熱設計、アジアマクロ、企業AIの費用規律を扱った。
・今朝の焦点は、AI需要そのものではない。
・焦点は、AI需要が利益と株式リターンへ変わる条件である。
・結論は明確。AI半導体需要は強い。
・ただし、投資では、メモリ不足、カスタムシリコン、電力、データセンター資本、企業AI採算、株式供給を同時に見る。

■メモリ不足はHBMだけで終わらない
・Reutersは、NVIDIAと韓国SKグループが6月8日に協力計画を説明する見通しを伝えた。
・記事時点では、協力の具体的な範囲は未開示である。
・HBM配分、価格、契約期間、前払い、顧客別数量は確認できない。
・重要なのは、Jensen Huang氏が不足を広く語った点である。
・不足はメモリ単体ではない。wafers、packaging、silicon photonicsを含む供給網全体の問題である。
・Vera Rubin向けHBM4では、Samsung、SK hynix、Micronが認定済みかつ生産中とされる。
・三社認定は前進である。ただし、供給制約の解消ではない。
・DRAM wafer、base die、2.5D実装、基板、熱、テスト、ラック統合は別に確認する。

■Samsung HBM4Eは一次資料として重要
・Samsungは、12層HBM4Eサンプルを主要顧客へ出荷し始めた。
・仕様は、48GB、14Gbps pin speed、最大16Gbps、最大3.6TB/s/stackである。
・同社は、1c DRAMと4nm logic base dieを使う。
・HBM4Eは、DRAM stackだけの製品ではない。
・base die、電源網、熱経路、先端パッケージング、顧客認定を含む複合部品である。
・ただし、発表はsample shipment段階である。
・量産月、採用顧客、出荷数量、ASP、歩留まり、NVIDIA向け配分は未確認である。
・Samsungの逆転や供給不足の解消は、まだ断定しない。

■メモリ価格は受益と原価圧力を同時に生む
・Morgan StanleyのMicronとSanDisk見通しは、AI半導体強気論の範囲が広がったことを示す。
・強気論はGPUとASICだけではない。
・DRAM、NAND、enterprise SSD、資本政策へ広がった。
・メモリ不足は、Micron、Samsung、SK hynix、SanDisk、Kioxiaの粗利とFCFには追い風になる。
・同じ不足は、クラウド、AIモデル企業、AIアプリ企業、PCメーカーには原価上昇として効く。
・価格上昇が強すぎると、需要側は設計を変える。
・候補は、KV cache圧縮、低容量構成、NAND/SSD階層化、推論専用ASIC、local/cloud routingである。
・韓国DCInsideでは、DDR5価格高騰でSRAMとDRAMの相対価格差が縮み、AIチップがon-chip SRAMを増やす仮説が議論された。
・ただし、SRAMは容量密度が低い。off-chip DRAMの単純な代替にはならない。
・この議論は数値の根拠ではない。
・価値は、メモリ原価がチップ設計とモデル効率化へ圧力をかける点にある。

■資本市場はAI実需と株式リターンを分ける
・Axiosは、AIビジネスの現実として、費用の大きさ、企業ROIの未達、Broadcom期待差、金利負担を挙げた。
・これはAI需要の否定ではない。
・需要が強くても、EPS、FCF、粗利、資本コスト、評価倍率へ変わるまでに条件がある。
・BroadcomのAI半導体売上は強い。
・市場は、7月期見通し、2027年以降のvisibility、gross margin、顧客集中、AI networkingのmixを同時に見る。
・Bloombergは、SpaceX、Anthropic、OpenAIの大型IPO候補と、AlphabetのAIデータセンター資本調達を扱った。
・AI capexを支える資金調達は、半導体需要の追い風である。
・同じ資金調達は、希薄化、株式供給増、指数リバランス、AI proxy銘柄の評価低下を生む。
・強い実需があっても、株価が同じ強さで動くとは限らない。

■AI computeは民間資本の商品になった
・米国VCと資産運用の発信では、AI computeがインフラ投資商品として扱われている。
・BlackstoneとGoogleのTPU Cloud JVは象徴的である。
・Blackstoneは50億ドルのinitial equity commitmentを行う。
・最初の500MW capacity onlineは2027年に見込まれる。
・TPUはGoogle内部のAI ASICだけではない。
・外部顧客向けcompute-as-a-serviceと、インフラ資本を組み合わせた商品になった。
・ARKは、data center systems investment、accelerated compute、custom silicon、training/inference costを一体で見る。
・Menloは、企業AI支出の拡大を示す。
・一方で、真のagent architectureはまだ少数である。
・多くの本番導入は、固定シーケンスとrouting中心である。
・VCとPEの発信は強気になりやすい。
・短期受注ではなく、民間資本がどの制約を収益機会と見ているかを見る。

■オープンモデルとカスタムシリコンは需要の形を変える
・open model ecosystemの焦点は、weights公開から広がった。
・焦点は、agent execution、post-training、evaluation、serving kernel、local deployment、roboticsへ移った。
・Hugging Faceのagent向けCLIは、agentがdataset、job、endpointを扱うときの失敗率とtoken消費を下げる。
・Holo3.1は、computer-use agentがlocal/edgeへ寄る可能性を示す。
・これはcloud GPU需要を消す材料ではない。
・低遅延や機密処理は端末側へ寄る。
・重いreasoning、評価、同期、ログ、モデル更新はクラウド側へ残る。
・Together AIとNous Researchの効率化は、長文context、KV cache、sparse attentionを軽くする。
・効率化はGPU需要を減らす場合もある。
・同じGPU fleetで処理できるtask量を増やす場合もある。
・AnthropicがOpenAI初期チップ人材を採用した動きも重要である。
・これは自社チップ発注の確定材料ではない。
・重要なのは、フロンティアAI企業が推論原価と単位エネルギーあたり性能を競争軸に入れた点である。

■朝刊の確認ポイント
・NVIDIAとSKの協力計画は、HBM契約だけで見ない。
・wafers、packaging、silicon photonics、韓国供給網まで範囲を確認する。
・Samsung HBM4Eは、sample shipmentと量産出荷を分ける。
・メモリ不足はメモリ企業の受益である。同時に、クラウドとAIモデル企業の原価圧力である。
・AI capexの強さは半導体需要を支える。
・ただし、金利、希薄化、株式供給増、proxy premium低下を確認する。
・open model、local agent、serving効率化、カスタムシリコンは、需要の場所と利益の取り分を変える。

speed、最大16Gbps、最大3.6TB/s/stackである。 ・同社は、1c DRAMと4nm logic base dieを使う。 ・HBM4Eは、DRAM stackだけの製品ではない。 ・base die、電源網、熱経路、先端パッケージング、顧客認定を含む複合部品である。 ・ただし、発表はsample shipment段階である。 ・量産月、採用顧客、出荷数量、ASP、歩留まり、NVIDIA向け配分は未確認である。 ・Samsungの逆転や供給不足の解消は、まだ断定しない。 ■メモリ価格は受益と原価圧力を同時に生む ・Morgan StanleyのMicronとSanDisk見通しは、AI半導体強気論の範囲が広がったことを示す。 ・強気論はGPUとASICだけではない。 ・DRAM、NAND、enterprise SSD、資本政策へ広がった。 ・メモリ不足は、Micron、Samsung、SK hynix、SanDisk、Kioxiaの粗利とFCFには追い風になる。 ・同じ不足は、クラウド、AIモデル企業、AIアプリ企業、PCメーカーには原価上昇として効く。 ・価格上昇が強すぎると、需要側は設計を変える。 ・候補は、KV cache圧縮、低容量構成、NAND/SSD階層化、推論専用ASIC、local/cloud routingである。 ・韓国DCInsideでは、DDR5価格高騰でSRAMとDRAMの相対価格差が縮み、AIチップがon-chip SRAMを増やす仮説が議論された。 ・ただし、SRAMは容量密度が低い。off-chip DRAMの単純な代替にはならない。 ・この議論は数値の根拠ではない。 ・価値は、メモリ原価がチップ設計とモデル効率化へ圧力をかける点にある。 ■資本市場はAI実需と株式リターンを分ける ・Axiosは、AIビジネスの現実として、費用の大きさ、企業ROIの未達、Broadcom期待差、金利負担を挙げた。 ・これはAI需要の否定ではない。 ・需要が強くても、EPS、FCF、粗利、資本コスト、評価倍率へ変わるまでに条件がある。 ・BroadcomのAI半導体売上は強い。 ・市場は、7月期見通し、2027年以降のvisibility、gross margin、顧客集中、AI networkingのmixを同時に見る。 ・Bloombergは、SpaceX、Anthropic、OpenAIの大型IPO候補と、AlphabetのAIデータセンター資本調達を扱った。 ・AI capexを支える資金調達は、半導体需要の追い風である。 ・同じ資金調達は、希薄化、株式供給増、指数リバランス、AI proxy銘柄の評価低下を生む。 ・強い実需があっても、株価が同じ強さで動くとは限らない。 ■AI computeは民間資本の商品になった ・米国VCと資産運用の発信では、AI computeがインフラ投資商品として扱われている。 ・BlackstoneとGoogleのTPU Cloud JVは象徴的である。 ・Blackstoneは50億ドルのinitial equity commitmentを行う。 ・最初の500MW capacity onlineは2027年に見込まれる。 ・TPUはGoogle内部のAI ASICだけではない。 ・外部顧客向けcompute-as-a-serviceと、インフラ資本を組み合わせた商品になった。 ・ARKは、data center systems investment、accelerated compute、custom silicon、training/inference costを一体で見る。 ・Menloは、企業AI支出の拡大を示す。 ・一方で、真のagent architectureはまだ少数である。 ・多くの本番導入は、固定シーケンスとrouting中心である。 ・VCとPEの発信は強気になりやすい。 ・短期受注ではなく、民間資本がどの制約を収益機会と見ているかを見る。 ■オープンモデルとカスタムシリコンは需要の形を変える ・open model ecosystemの焦点は、weights公開から広がった。 ・焦点は、agent execution、post-training、evaluation、serving kernel、local deployment、roboticsへ移った。 ・Hugging Faceのagent向けCLIは、agentがdataset、job、endpointを扱うときの失敗率とtoken消費を下げる。 ・Holo3.1は、computer-use agentがlocal/edgeへ寄る可能性を示す。 ・これはcloud GPU需要を消す材料ではない。 ・低遅延や機密処理は端末側へ寄る。 ・重いreasoning、評価、同期、ログ、モデル更新はクラウド側へ残る。 ・Together AIとNous Researchの効率化は、長文context、KV cache、sparse attentionを軽くする。 ・効率化はGPU需要を減らす場合もある。 ・同じGPU fleetで処理できるtask量を増やす場合もある。 ・AnthropicがOpenAI初期チップ人材を採用した動きも重要である。 ・これは自社チップ発注の確定材料ではない。 ・重要なのは、フロンティアAI企業が推論原価と単位エネルギーあたり性能を競争軸に入れた点である。 ■朝刊の確認ポイント ・NVIDIAとSKの協力計画は、HBM契約だけで見ない。 ・wafers、packaging、silicon photonics、韓国供給網まで範囲を確認する。 ・Samsung HBM4Eは、sample shipmentと量産出荷を分ける。 ・メモリ不足はメモリ企業の受益である。同時に、クラウドとAIモデル企業の原価圧力である。 ・AI capexの強さは半導体需要を支える。 ・ただし、金利、希薄化、株式供給増、proxy premium低下を確認する。 ・open model、local agent、serving効率化、カスタムシリコンは、需要の場所と利益の取り分を変える。"}}}, "rest_id": "2063772571473244583", "source": "20260519", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "958", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

So let me do the engineering for you, since clearly nobody on here will. The bottleneck was never can you make light go through a waveguide. It’s all fucking thermals which is downstream of packaging. Specifically, how do you get a photonic engine onto the same substrate as a switch ASIC or XPU without your yield falling off a cliff and your reliability failing. TSMC’s answer is CoWoS where they bolt everything onto one big monolithic silicon interposer. Cute, until you hit the reticle limit and start duct-taping interposers together (CoWoS-S, then -R, then -L, soon -PleaseStop). Every chiplet and HBM stack you add to that single interposer compounds your defect probability and one bad die leads to a five-figure package going into the dumpster. CoWoS is thermally retarded and the whole industry knows this and it’s why capacity “can’t expand” and Jensen is acting like a bouncer in the front of a club choosing who gets pass the velvet rope. There is ONLY one company that will make copackaged optics work and expand in the rack… it’s not Lumentum, it’s not Coherent, it’s fucking INTELLLL. Intel’s EMIB gets rid of a giant reticle limited interposer and replaces it with a tiny silicon bridge that does the high-density coupling locally, exactly where you need it. You localize the hard part and the thermals in one area and your yield is ridiculously high. Comparing EMIB & CoWoS is so funny cause EMIB is north of 95% yield with like 12 reticle size equivalent package while CoWoS falls off a cliff after 5.5 reticles it’s that bad…now imagine adding thermally sensitive photonics. People don’t know this but Intel has been doing silicon photonics in-house for ~25 years... In 2024 they showed an Optical I/O chiplet doing 2 Tbps bidirectionally at ~5 pJ/bit, with the PIC and EIC co-packaged right against the ASIC and it’s all because of EMIB. And even more critically than that, they’ve actually run the fiber-attach and reliability/test flow to JEDEC-grade standards already, which everyone hand-waves until their links flap in production. My prediction is clear: Intel will capture over 90% of the copackaged silicon photonics market in the next five years because there is NO ALTERNATIVE."}}}, "rest_id": "2063735227382677573", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "277705", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2063770017519239413", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "17523", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

So let me do the engineering for you, since clearly nobody on here will. The bottleneck was never can you make light go through a waveguide. It’s all fucking thermals which is downstream of packaging. Specifically, how do you get a photonic engine onto the same substrate as a switch ASIC or XPU without your yield falling off a cliff and your reliability failing. TSMC’s answer is CoWoS where they bolt everything onto one big monolithic silicon interposer. Cute, until you hit the reticle limit and start duct-taping interposers together (CoWoS-S, then -R, then -L, soon -PleaseStop). Every chiplet and HBM stack you add to that single interposer compounds your defect probability and one bad die leads to a five-figure package going into the dumpster. CoWoS is thermally retarded and the whole industry knows this and it’s why capacity “can’t expand” and Jensen is acting like a bouncer in the front of a club choosing who gets pass the velvet rope. There is ONLY one company that will make copackaged optics work and expand in the rack… it’s not Lumentum, it’s not Coherent, it’s fucking INTELLLL. Intel’s EMIB gets rid of a giant reticle limited interposer and replaces it with a tiny silicon bridge that does the high-density coupling locally, exactly where you need it. You localize the hard part and the thermals in one area and your yield is ridiculously high. Comparing EMIB & CoWoS is so funny cause EMIB is north of 95% yield with like 12 reticle size equivalent package while CoWoS falls off a cliff after 5.5 reticles it’s that bad…now imagine adding thermally sensitive photonics. People don’t know this but Intel has been doing silicon photonics in-house for ~25 years... In 2024 they showed an Optical I/O chiplet doing 2 Tbps bidirectionally at ~5 pJ/bit, with the PIC and EIC co-packaged right against the ASIC and it’s all because of EMIB. And even more critically than that, they’ve actually run the fiber-attach and reliability/test flow to JEDEC-grade standards already, which everyone hand-waves until their links flap in production. My prediction is clear: Intel will capture over 90% of the copackaged silicon photonics market in the next five years because there is NO ALTERNATIVE."}}}, "rest_id": "2063735227382677573", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "277705", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2063766017411887492", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "10438", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
How this is positive for $MRVL according to Ai

The genuine read-through for Marvell is the platform validation. SK Hynix is codeveloping memory for NVIDIA Vera Rubin AI supercomputers, Vera CPUs, RTX Spark-powered PCs and Jetson Thor platforms . Marvell already has its own NVIDIA tie-in: back in March 2026, NVIDIA and Marvell announced a strategic partnership connecting Marvell to the NVIDIA AI factory ecosystem through NVLink Fusion, and NVIDIA invested $2 billion in Marvell . Marvell provides custom XPUs and NVLink Fusion–compatible scale-up networking, while NVIDIA provides the Vera CPU, ConnectX NICs, Bluefield DPUs, NVLink interconnect and Spectrum-X switches . So anything that accelerates the Vera Rubin / AI-factory buildout broadly supports demand for the custom silicon, optical interconnect, and networking content Marvell sells into those same racks.
Notably, Marvell’s own IP portfolio includes custom high-bandwidth memory (HBM), silicon photonics, optical IO, and compute fabric interfaces like PCIe Gen 7  — so a healthier, faster-scaling memory supply chain feeding NVIDIA platforms is a tailwind for the systems Marvell co-designs into.
The honest counterpoints: this is a memory-maker deal, and Marvell doesn’t make DRAM/HBM cells — it integrates custom HBM into XPUs, so there’s no direct revenue line here. If anything, the bearish framing is that NVIDIA continues to deepen first-party and Korean-memory relationships, and Marvell’s bigger swing factor remains its custom XPU design wins with hyperscalers, not NVIDIA’s memory sourcing. Marvell already guided strongly on its own — it raised its fiscal 2027 and 2028 revenue outlook on exceptional AI bookings across 800G/1.6T optics, Ethernet switches, scale-up optical, DCI modules, and custom XPU solutions  — so this announcement doesn’t change Marvell’s fundamentals.
Bottom line: mildly positive sentiment read-through (validates the AI-factory/Vera Rubin ramp Marvell is levered to)

text:
I've been looking into ZBLAN optical fiber as it's one of the clearer cases for manufacturing in space. 

ZBLAN is a heavy metal fluoride glass fiber. It offers the potential for lower optical loss than conventional silica fiber and transmits across a broader wavelength range into the mid-infrared. These traits could support longer transmission distances and new uses in sensing and communications.

On Earth, producing high-quality ZBLAN has been difficult. Gravity induces convection in the molten glass during the drawing process leads to microscopic crystals and defects that scatter light and limit performance.

In microgravity, these issues are reduced. The glass stays more homogeneous during drawing. Recent work by Flawless Photonics on the International Space Station produced over 11 kilometers of fiber in about a month.

The returned samples are under analysis, and microscope comparisons show much cleaner microstructures than Earth-produced fiber.

Challenges remain. ZBLAN is more brittle than silica and has a larger minimum bend radius, which affects handling. The technology is still in the demonstration phase but commercial production is being planned,and the plan is to manufacture it in space.

text: I think volatility will be the theme this summer. Lot of great potential swings both ways in many sectors. The more we can trade, the better the market, and the healthier speculation is the healthier the market will be. 4007}, {"from_index": 4787, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 4792}, {"from_index": 4831, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 4847}]}, "text": "Copper can't keep up with AI. That's not an opinion, it's physics. Every data center being built right now is replacing electrical connections with light. NVIDIA confirmed it with $4.5 billion in direct investment. I mapped 25 public companies across the photonics value chain: Every AI cluster being built today hits the same wall. A hundred thousand GPUs mean nothing if the data can't move between them fast enough. Copper maxed out years ago and photonics replaced it: lasers, optical fiber, and transceivers that push data at the speed of light. The AI transceiver market doubled in two years. NVIDIA committed $4.5 billion across three photonics companies this year alone. This is where the infrastructure money is going. Here's the full value chain: 🔬 MATERIALS & WAFERS This is the bottom of the chain. Every laser and transceiver starts as a wafer substrate: indium phosphide, gallium arsenide, germanium, specialty glass. Nobody above this layer can produce anything without these inputs, and right now the most critical one, indium phosphide, is the tightest material in the entire AI supply chain. The gap between demand and capacity is getting worse, not better. I think this is the most asymmetric layer on the map. Investors chase the transceiver companies and ignore who grows the substrates underneath them. But NVIDIA is writing checks worth billions in cash and warrants to lock up supply from this exact layer. First link in the chain, last to get attention, and the one that chokes everything above it if it breaks. Tickers: $GLW, $AXTI, $IQE, $AIXA, $AMS 💡 CORE PHOTONIC DEVICES This layer converts electricity into light and back. Without it, zero data moves through fiber. NVIDIA dropped $4 billion into two companies here this year just to secure production capacity, and both of them joined the S&P 500 within weeks of each other. That should tell you how fast this went from niche to essential. The supply gap is not closing. The companies shipping next gen lasers at volume can be counted on one hand, and switching suppliers takes years of requalification. Order books stretch past twelve months. Every next generation GPU cluster consumes more of these components than the last, and no one can substitute them on short notice. I watch this layer more closely than any other. Tickers: $IPGP, $COHR, $LITE, $LASR, $SIVE 🔌 COMPONENTS & MODULES The companies here take raw lasers and detectors, package them into finished transceivers and modules, and ship them straight to hyperscalers. If the layers below are the engine, this is the vehicle that actually reaches the customer. Hyperscaler purchase orders land here. The revenue acceleration shows up here first. What I like about this layer is that you can underwrite it today, not in two years. These are businesses with signed capacity commitments and product already moving. The consolidation angle matters too: larger photonics players have already started absorbing standalone module companies, and whoever remains independent gains pricing power as options thin out. Tickers: $AAOI, $MTSI, $VIAV, $LPTH ⚙️ SYSTEMS & EQUIPMENT No company above this layer can manufacture a single photonic component without the machines built here. One of these names holds 100% of the EUV lithography market with zero competitors. Others supply the bonding equipment for co packaged optics or the process control instruments used across the majority of advanced packaging lines. If photonics is the gold rush, this is the layer selling the picks. My honest take: this is where the smart, patient capital parks. Equipment companies have pricing power and multi year order books that generate cash through full capex cycles. They attract holders who don't panic on the first pullback. The stocks don't run 1,000% overnight, but they compound while everything above them swings, and that tradeoff is worth more than most people give it credit for. Tickers: $ASML, $BESI, $ASM, $LPKF, $MKSI 🔍 TEST, METROLOGY & YIELD The most ignored layer on this map, and arguably the one with the cleanest business model. Every wafer, laser, and transceiver has to be tested and verified before it ships. As speeds climb and photonic devices get more complex, the testing challenge compounds fast. The industry is now constrained not only by what it can build but by what it can prove actually works. Yield is money. Better defect detection means better margins for every company upstream, which is why foundries keep buying test equipment even when they slash budgets everywhere else. These are capital light businesses tied to every unit of production across the chain. Last check before product hits the customer, and one of the few layers where demand doesn't cycle down when the rest of semis softens. Tickers: $CAMT, $FORM, $AEHR, $ONTO, $VIAV 🧠FINAL THOUGHTS The NVIDIA capital concentration tells the whole story. One company wrote $4.5 billion in checks to three photonics suppliers in a single quarter. That is a company locking down the one input that could bottleneck its GPU deployments: the optical interconnect. Returns across this sector have been historic over the past twelve months. But separate the revenue growers from the narrative trades. Some of these companies are printing real quarterly numbers that would impress in any sector. Others are carrying multi billion dollar market caps on sub $100 million in annual revenue. Same sector, wildly different risk. Every generation of AI infrastructure from here forward needs more photonics. Not less. The copper to light transition inside data centers is early. Co packaged optics is barely in deployment, and 1.6T transceivers are ramping with 3.2T already on roadmaps. The chain locks together: stress on any single link reprices every link above it."}}}, "rest_id": "2062502220889837836", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "266426", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2062636370233831773", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1069", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
1.6T optical is the next interconnect generation, and  it’s blowing up because the rack-scale GPU/XPU clusters being built in 2026–27 finally need that bandwidth — and the volume inflection is hitting right now. 

1.6T needs 200G/lane SerDes, 1.6T DSPs, and the laser/optics (EML, CW for silicon photonics) to all be production-ready together. That convergence is happening in the 2026 window, which is why it’s inflecting now rather than gradually.

text:
$LITE - LUMENTUM GAVE BACK 20% FROM ITS PEAK ON ZERO COMPANY NEWS — THE OPTICS TRADE IS JUST DIGESTING

LITE fell ~10% today and is now down more than 20% from its recent high, but there's no company-specific catalyst — it's a sector-wide pullback across photonics names after a parabolic AI-optical run. Analysts still carry a Buy consensus, and demand for optical interconnects in AI data centers keeps building.

The structural case is the co-design problem: at hyperscale, moving a bit over copper now costs more energy than the compute it carries, so the interconnect flips to light. That's why co-packaged optics and external laser sources are the next bottleneck — and Lumentum sits on the laser and transceiver supply side of exactly that shift. The pullback is positioning unwinding, not a thesis break.

text:
$LWLG signed a real deal with a real foundry, and the stock is up around 250% this year on it. Here's the part the hype threads skip: it still has essentially no revenue.

A development agreement is a tryout, not a contract. The two get conflated on purpose.

The bull pitch is the "Qualcomm model": Lightwave's polymer modulator tech gets baked into Tower Semiconductor's photonics platform, every future customer becomes a potential royalty stream, fat margins someday. It's a genuinely elegant story. The problem is that every load-bearing word is in the future tense.

Targeting royalties. Years from tapeout. Someday at scale.

What exists today is a development deal, a handful of customers in late-stage prototyping, a list of earlier-stage maybes, and a roughly $730 million market cap pricing in a royalty river that hasn't produced a meaningful dollar. Photonics is real and important to AI; that doesn't make this specific microcap de-risked.

A great narrative and a pre-revenue balance sheet can coexist for a long time.

The tell is the recent action: down about 20% in a month even while up 250% on the year, which is what a hope-driven stock does, lurching on sentiment because there are no earnings to anchor it. The deal de-risked the science. It did not turn the science into income.

The technology might win. The stock is still betting on a future that hasn't shipped.

text:
$LWLG signed a real deal with a real foundry, and the stock is up around 250% this year on it. Here's the part the hype threads skip: it still has essentially no revenue.

A development agreement is a tryout, not a contract. The two get conflated on purpose.

The bull pitch is the "Qualcomm model": Lightwave's polymer modulator tech gets baked into Tower Semiconductor's photonics platform, every future customer becomes a potential royalty stream, fat margins someday. It's a genuinely elegant story. The problem is that every load-bearing word is in the future tense.

Targeting royalties. Years from tapeout. Someday at scale.

What exists today is a development deal, a handful of customers in late-stage prototyping, a list of earlier-stage maybes, and a roughly $730 million market cap pricing in a royalty river that hasn't produced a meaningful dollar. Photonics is real and important to AI; that doesn't make this specific microcap de-risked.

A great narrative and a pre-revenue balance sheet can coexist for a long time.

The tell is the recent action: down about 20% in a month even while up 250% on the year, which is what a hope-driven stock does, lurching on sentiment because there are no earnings to anchor it. The deal de-risked the science. It did not turn the science into income.

The technology might win. The stock is still betting on a future that hasn't shipped.

text:
There's now an ETF for photonics and optics, ticker $LYTE. When a once-obscure corner of tech gets its own ticker, the theme has officially gone mainstream.

That's a signal, and it cuts both ways.

The fair version of the bull case is real: moving data inside AI data centers with light instead of electrons is becoming a genuine bottleneck, and optical components are quietly essential to scaling the whole buildout. Packaging that into one fund lets ordinary investors bet the picks-and-shovels layer under AI without picking one fragile small-cap.

Light is becoming the wiring of the AI era. Someone built a basket for it.

The caution is timing. ETFs tend to launch after a narrative is hot, near the part of the cycle where the easy money already moved. The fund exists because demand for the story exists, and demand for a story usually peaks before the story does.

The product is a thermometer for the hype, not just the trend.

So treat it as both. Photonics genuinely matters to AI infrastructure, and the existence of a dedicated ETF tells you a lot of people already figured that out. Real theme, possibly late entry.

The light's real. Whether you're early to it is the part the ticker can't tell you.

text:
Nokia is bolting Nvidia GPUs directly into its cell towers, and the reason is a number: 200 milliseconds.

A factory robot can't wait that long for a cloud server to think. So the thinking moves to the tower.

This is the edge-computing thesis finally getting concrete. Instead of routing every AI decision to a distant data center and back, $NOK turns base stations into tiny compute nodes, running the inference where the signal already is. T-Mobile was the first US carrier to pilot it. Nvidia's CEO described a future where every radio network becomes an AI network.

The latency you can't engineer away is the latency of distance.

Underneath the headline, Nokia quietly rebuilt itself. Its $2.3 billion purchase of Infinera turned it from a reseller of someone else's photonics into a vertically integrated optical company with its own chips.

Optical networks grew 20% year over year last quarter. Management raised infrastructure guidance into the teens.

The unglamorous telecom name nobody discussed for a decade is positioning as connective tissue for AI at the edge. Whether the AI-RAN vision pays off is unproven, and the capex is real. But the bet is specific.

The cloud got all the attention. The tower is where the robots actually live.

text:
❗ONLY 9 Hours Left❗

[Last Call for Substack/Full article]

Pricing update — effective June 3, 2026 (11:59PM Pacific Time)

PhotonCap started with a simple bet: that there's demand for silicon photonics research that goes deeper than press releases and earnings recaps.

That bet has paid off.

What PhotonCap does differently:

This publication doesn't rely on a single source. Academic conference papers, company PRs, earnings calls, and industry news are cross-referenced and analyzed together. That's how you catch what others miss: when a PR tells one story but the paper tells another.

Here's what that looks like in practice:

Primary source analysis — academic conference papers and journal publications dissected through an investor's lens. Not academic summaries — what each breakthrough means for the supply chain and who captures the value.

Investment maps — supply-chain-level deep dives mapping who actually makes what, from laser sources to optical packaging. The kind of analysis you won't find in sell-side reports.

Portfolio and earnings — portfolio holdings shared on a quarterly or semi-annual basis, alongside earnings analysis that decodes what the numbers actually mean at the product level.

PC101 lecture series — free foundational lectures on silicon photonics, from waveguide physics to PIC design, in English and Korean.

From device physics to public markets — PhotonCap connects the layers that most research treats separately.

Where we stand — and where we're going:

PhotonCap has grown to well over a thousand paid subscribers and ranks #22 on the Substack Top Bestseller list. I'm grateful that this publication is now trusted not only by individual investors, but by institutional investors, hedge funds, and sell-side analysts — as well as fellow research publications including Citrini Research, FundaAI, ChipStrat, Vik's Newsletter, and Jason's Chip, who subscribe as paid members.

Going forward, PhotonCap will expand beyond photonics and optics to cover a broader range of companies — all through the same engineering-first lens. The approach stays the same: primary sources, physics-grounded analysis, and supply chain mapping. The scope gets wider.

New pricing — effective June 3 (11:59 PM, Pacific Time):

Monthly: $50/month (currently $30)

Annual: $500/year (currently $300)

Already a paid subscriber? Your rate is locked in permanently. Nothing changes for you.

If you've been considering subscribing, now is the time. Lock in the current rate before June 3.

Content offerings, format, and frequency may evolve over time as PhotonCap grows.

— PhotonCap

text:
-> IP acquisition
-> Just waiting for CPO to take off

$SIVE is a laser chokepoint for photonics and are publicly validated by:

- $GFS (1 of 2 public laser suppliers with $LITE for CPO per presentation)

- $JBL (“Relatively dramatic moat” for pluggables built with Sivers)

- CHIPS ACT for the overall company

For highest visibility:

-> Ayar is the largest CPO player that primary sources $SIVE and are expected to ramp in 2027.

-> $POET is another near term CPO volume player that’s heavily visible with Sivers.

For OSINT mapping:

$MRVL Celestial (direct, not through Poet), Lightmatter, Lightelligence, were all high confidence customers of Sivers. 

$SIVE is also developing/qualifying with multiple more optical transceiver players following Jabil.

It feels like they’re going to end up everywhere. 

I’m not sure people realize how special that many qualifications is coming from a <$2B MC laser company is… right before 2027 volume ramp. 

Especially while all the other laser companies trade at $15B-$70B valuations. 

Just need pluggables to bridge revenue gap into H2 2027 (CPO Scale up)

Then making every laser they sell more valuable following the $LITE playbook, to capture more TAM of both markets.  

- In the overarching optical $141B TAM (10x) in the next 1 1/2 years. (Goldman Sachs)

- and CPO TAM going from 0 to $81B in the next 1 1/2 years. 

So, easily multiply revenue opportunity overnight doing IP acquisition downstream. 

It’s more of just a waiting game, I think $SIVE is very undervalued relative to forward revenue potential. 

If it were a private Silicon Valley startup it would probably be worth $4-6B today.

Just needs to get listed on NASDAQ for premiums to bridge that gap.

text:
$SIVE CEO: “In the last 4-5 months we have received a lot of photonics opportunities to the level where both businesses (wireless and photonics) are challenging each other in terms of pipeline they occupy.”

In the past it was more focused on wireless. Now it isn’t. 👀

He also states that pipeline opportunities are at different stages. That’s why they are key to future revenue potential, as they are closely tied to execution - not just open numbers without a real path to production. 😉

text:
Wall Street is still treating $STM like a legacy, cyclical automotive chipmaker. That narrative is completely broken. Buried beneath short-term restructuring costs and an elevated trailing multiple is a massive structural pivot into AI silicon photonics—quietly anchored by a newly disclosed warrant agreement with Amazon. While the market fixates on temporary factory underutilization, it is entirely missing a multi-billion dollar datacenter expansion. Here is the real investment thesis. 🌐🔬 #Semiconductors

text:
What’s so impressive to me about @joinautopilot’s “Photonics is Next” basket is that @michaelsikand + @KawzInvests & the folks at Autopilot were months ahead of the ETF wrappers now chasing the theme. $LYTE Photonics ETF from @roundhill is not yet listed, and $FOTO Photonics ETF just listed on May 29th. 

Before either existed, @joinautopilot provided the best pure-play photonics ETF exposure you could actually get. $EUV is the closest listed alternative, but it’s not a true pure play. It’s thematically diluted with semicap and foundry names like $TSM, $ASML, etc. 

By the time $LYTE finally lists, a lot of the alpha will likely already be gone. The growth is happening before the ETF shows up, not after.
Check $LITE & $AAOI YTD gain for reference.

Autopilot has proved it’s a serious platform for retail investors, not only a Congress copy-trading app everyone knows it for. Haven’t ever used Autopilot before, but I’m now open to the possibility. 

Bravo, @joinautopilot @michaelsikand @KawzInvests 🫰🫰🫰

(Appreciation post, not a paid promo)

text:
$sive CEO 인터뷰

1. 1분기 실적

- 환율과 미국 정부 셧다운 여파(정부 자금 및 국방 지출 승인 지연)로 인해 자체 목표보다 다소 아쉬운 실적을 기록함.

2. 사업 현황

- 현재 성장을 이끄는 무선(Wireless) 부문은 위성 통신 시장 확대를 준비 중이며, 미래 동력인 포토닉스(Photonics) 부문은 2027년 AI 데이터 센터 시장 진입을 준비하고 있음. 

3. 미국 상장 추진

- 미국 증시 교차 상장을 준비 중
- 규제와 비용 부담이 늘지만, 대규모 전문 투자자 확보와 장기적 자금 조달에 유리하며 이를 위해 재무 통제와 현장 인력 투자를 늘렸음.

4. 최종 목표

- 다수의 신제품이 본격적으로 대량 양산되는 2027년을 제품 폭발 성장의 해로 만드는 것이 현재 최우선 과제임.

Software - $PLTR, $NOW, $CRM, $RDDT, $AXON etc.
Photonics - $LITE, $COHR, $AAOI etc.
Drone - $ONDS, $KTOS, $RCAT, $AVAV etc.
Fintech - $SOFI, $HOOD, $COIN etc.
Crypto - $MSTR, $BMNR, $GLXY etc.
Rare earth - $MP, $USAR, etc.

text:
📊 Top stocks on LunarCrush right now: who's moving and why.

These are ranked by LunarCrush AltRank, which measures social attention and market performance across the entire stock market. The data breaks into three groups today. 

1️⃣ AI, space, and defense names with real catalysts 
2️⃣ Biotech and health plays moving on deals and data 
3️⃣ A name-mixup trap inflating one struggling stock

Reddit $RDDT +8.92% 
Sits at #1. People are talking up its AI data-licensing deals as a real money source, pushing its share of the stock conversation to almost 5x its normal level. Sentiment is only 62% though, with analyst downgrades pushing back.

GRAIL $GRAL +10.15% 
The cancer-screening company caught a bid on partnership rumors ahead of new trial data. One caveat: a lot of the "Grail" chatter is actually about fashion resale and Pokemon "grails," so the real signal is smaller than the number looks.

Alight $ALIT +8.38% 
Be careful with this one. Almost all the buzz is about Alight Motion, a video-editing app, not the actual HR company. The stock itself is down on the week with delisting rumors hanging over it, so the high rank is mostly a name mix-up.

Abivax $ABVX +16.72% 
This biotech popped on takeover rumors with key trial data due in Q2. Mentions and engagement hit all-time highs, but the move is speculation-driven, and fears of the company raising money are capping how far bulls will chase it.

Redwire $RDW +17.59% 
A space and defense company riding SpaceX IPO excitement and a growing pile of military contracts. It's up about 129% on the month at 86% sentiment, though some worry about it issuing new shares.

Applied Optoelectronics $AAOI +11.81% 
The clearest AI-hardware story on the board. It makes the optical parts data centers need, and traders see a real demand "supercycle" as big cloud players ramp orders. Strong 84% sentiment.

Laser Photonics $LASE +9.74% 
The wild card. It's up over 300% in a week after the U.S. military validated its anti-drone laser system, and trading volume exploded about 30x. But it's a tiny company, so expect sharp swings both ways.

Oscar Health $OSCR +16.34% 
The health-insurance tech name hit a 52-week high on a strong earnings report and insiders buying their own stock. Sentiment is 90%, with the bull case built on a solid cash pile and real growth.

BlackBerry $BB +6.66% 
The old phone brand is now an AI-software story. A new stock buyback and its QNX car-software business sent it to fresh highs, up about 80% on the month. Some doubters still bring up its decline years.

Ouster $OUST +7.93% 
A LiDAR and robotics-sensing company that broke out on a new product line called Rev8 and a FieldAI partnership. It's up about 118% on the month at 84% sentiment, riding the robots-and-automation theme.

text:
The recent buzz surrounding Marvell Technology $MRVL becoming a potential trillion-dollar company stems largely from a high-profile endorsement by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang at the Computex 2026 trade show. The analysts and industry observers point to several fundamental pillars that support this ambitious valuation thesis:
1 Essential Data Center Connectivity: As AI workloads become disaggregated and distributed across thousands of chips, connectivity has become the primary bottleneck. Marvell provides the essential networking and connectivity chips that allow these massive clusters to communicate at the high speeds required for modern AI.  

2 Strategic Nvidia Partnership: Nvidia, which has a deep interest in the success of the AI ecosystem, recently invested $2 billion in $MRVL. This partnership, centered on NVLink Fusion, aims to integrate their technologies to build more efficient AI infrastructure, essentially making Marvell a critical partner rather than just a vendor.  

3 Leadership in Custom Silicon (ASICs): Marvell is a leader in custom-designed chips for major hyperscalers like Amazon and Microsoft. Unlike general-purpose GPUs, these ASICs are tailored for specific tasks, creating sticky, multi-year revenue streams that provide a massive, predictable growth foundation.  

4 Optical Networking Expertise: Marvell is at the forefront of the shift toward optical interconnects, which use light instead of electricity to move data. This technology is critical for scaling AI performance as bandwidth demands continue to explode.  

5 Industry-Leading Product Innovation: The company recently launched the Teralynx T100, an industry-first 102.4 Tbps switch designed specifically to address latency and energy constraints in AI data centers, keeping them ahead of competitors.  

6 Rapidly Expanding AI Infrastructure Revenue: Data center operations now drive roughly 74% of Marvell's total revenue. The company has projected strong, sustained double-digit growth in this segment, fueled by the global buildout of AI data centers.  

7 Aggressive M&A Strategy: Marvell has been strategically acquiring startups like Celestial AI and XConn to bolster its capabilities in advanced packaging and optical interconnects, allowing it to quickly fill gaps in its technological roadmap.  

8 Silicon Photonics Potential: Marvell is heavily investing in silicon photonics, a technology that promises to significantly improve the energy efficiency and data transfer speeds of future AI clusters. This is widely considered a "real" long-term growth driver.

9 One-Stop Shop Positioning: By offering a comprehensive suite of solutions, from processors and ASICs to optical I/O and switching. Marvell is positioning itself as a vital, end-to-end infrastructure provider for the cloud-scale AI era.  

10 Market Momentum and Valuation Re-rating: Following Jensen Huang's endorsement, the market has begun to aggressively re-price Marvell, reflecting the expectation that it will capture a massive share of the AI hardware market in the coming years. This investor confidence acts as a catalyst for the capital influx needed to support a larger market cap.

text:
$MRVL Marvell Technology: Architect of the AI Connectivity Backbone. Investment Thesis.

Marvell Technology has completed a strategic transformation that few legacy semiconductor companies execute cleanly. The pivot away from traditional networking and storage semiconductors toward custom silicon and high-speed connectivity for AI data center infrastructure is not a repositioning in progress — it's largely done, and the financial trajectory is beginning to reflect it. The Celestial AI acquisition extends that positioning into optical interconnects and photonic fabrics, addressing the next layer of AI infrastructure bottleneck where copper interconnects hit physical limits at scale.

The hyperscaler demand dynamic is the engine behind the revenue ramp thesis. Custom ASIC design wins with major cloud providers represent a different category of customer relationship than merchant silicon sales — they create deep technical integration, long program lifecycles, and switching costs that are measured in years of co-development rather than procurement cycles. That stickiness is a genuine competitive moat, but it also concentrates revenue in ways that make the business sensitive to program-level decisions at a small number of accounts.

Matthew Murphy's leadership has been the consistent thread through the transformation. The strategic decisions — which legacy businesses to exit, which adjacencies to pursue, which acquisitions to make — reflect a coherent vision that has been executed with unusual discipline for a company managing simultaneous portfolio transitions. That track record matters when evaluating whether the optical interconnect bet will be executed with similar precision.

Technical execution at advanced nodes is the capability risk that the strategic narrative doesn't eliminate. Custom silicon at leading-edge process nodes requires sustained investment in design talent, EDA infrastructure, and customer co-development resources. The competitive environment for that talent — against hyperscalers building internal silicon teams and peers targeting the same design win opportunities — is intensifying, which adds cost pressure alongside the execution complexity.

The valuation is the honest tension in the thesis. Marvell is priced as a confirmed AI infrastructure compounder, and the revenue ramp forecasts embedded in the current multiple require hyperscaler demand to remain elevated, program ramps to execute on schedule, and optical interconnect adoption to accelerate at the pace the Celestial AI acquisition assumes. Each of those is plausible individually — together, they represent a high bar that leaves the stock exposed to any single point of execution friction. The strategic positioning is among the strongest in the AI semiconductor supply chain; the question is how much of that quality is already in the price.

- AI bubble will pop. Infra/photonics/energy buildout will remain to power the next AI wave.

text:
Here are 5 AI-related stocks I am looking at for 2026-7

AR WEARABLES & DISPLAY $KEEL 

Kopin Corporation. AI/AR Wearables Play. Bullish technical pattern crossing the 50-day MA. Direct catalyst with military contracts for lightweight display tech.

OPTICAL INTERCONNECT & TRANSCEIVERS $POET

POET Technologies. Optical Interconnect Leader. Essential silicon photonics components for next-gen data centers. Perfect play for the 'Memory & Optical' bottleneck. 

Applied Optoelectronics.  $AAOI
 
Optical transceivers for data center buildouts. Demand is high with strong manufacturing scalability. Low valuation compared to legacy optical plays.

RF, MILLIMETER WAVE & NETWORKING $SIVEF

Sivers Semiconductors. RF & Millimeter wave solutions. Crucial infrastructure for 5G/AI network architecture and satellite communications. Moving average crossover with strong volume.

AI CLOUD & GPU INFRASTRUCTURE $NEBIUS

Nebius Group. AI Cloud & GPU infrastructure. Emerging European player acting as a high-growth alternative to CoreWeave and Applied Digital. Proximity to key tech hubs gives them a massive edge.

text:
$CRDO Fundamental Deep Dive 🟢 Credo Tech

OVERVIEW

Credo operates as a hyper-growth high-speed connectivity semiconductor provider that has a broad portfolio of products spanning SerDes, optical DSPs, Active Electrical Cables (AECs), and silicon photonics solutions. FY2025 proved their explosive growth narrative, achieving an annual revenue of $1.34B, growing +206% YoY. The AI/HPC data center segment delivered the bulk of growth, driven by hyperscaler demand, AI cluster infrastructure, and optical/electrical interconnects (800G/1.6T).

$CRDO is the dominant force in delivering copper architecture through its market-leading AECs. Low-power, high-reliability copper cables that facilitate short-reach intra-rack and spine connections in AI clusters today. Over the coming years, the industry will transition toward photonics for longer-reach, higher-density interconnects at 1.6T+ speeds, where copper hits physical limits. The fear with this development was that Credo would be essentially phased out of the market with time, leading to the massive correction in early 2026.

However, the recent DustPhotonics acquisition (completed in May 2026) brings in-house silicon photonics PIC technology for 800G/1.6T/3.2T+ NPO and CPO, solidifying its future fully vertically integrated stack (SerDes + DSP + silicon photonics + system integration). This positions Credo perfectly for the next decade of AI infrastructure evolution, capturing copper today and optics tomorrow.

In my eyes, This company is a premier "picks-and-shovels" play on the connectivity buildout. Margins are disgustingly high (60%+ gross) now and in the years moving forward with a strong FCF + balance sheet, providing the company a leg-up on competition due to minimal capital intensity given their fabless model. These margins are expected to be sustained or even expand further as higher-ASP optical and silicon photonics products ramp.

--------------------------

GROWTH OUTLOOK

$CRDO has consistently outperformed expectations without sandbagging guidance. Management projects robust revenue growth over the next 3–5 years, driven largely by AI connectivity + photonics acceleration.

Credo maintains deep partnerships with all major hyperscalers (including $MSFT, $AMZN, $META, $ORCL and xAI), a fact known due to their famous "purple cables" acting as visual proof across imaged AI/HPC builds. Their solutions are currently powering xAI’s Colossus cluster via ZeroFlap AECs to maximize GPU utilization and cluster uptime. This is the part many sleep on! These existing relationships give Credo massive leverage to drive future growth when hyperscalers are primed to adopt the new 1.6T+ optical DSPs and silicon photonics products as the transition accelerates.

The AI connectivity segment projects strong growth of ~80%+ YoY in FY26 (with a re-inflection in this strength in the back half of 2027) due to expanded hyperscaler/AI focus. Over FY2026, the company is projected to report ~$2.4B Revenue and ~$6 EPS. I positioned before this inflection starts in March. I am thankful for the opportunity we were provided.

This growth fuels income statement expansion, with non-GAAP gross margins holding steady in the mid-to-high 60s% and operating margins nearing 45-50% as higher-margin optical and photonics products enter the mix further down the line, driving EPS sharply higher. The balance sheet is pristine, currently holding $1.4B in cash with strong FCF generation and zero debt thanks to the fabless model. Product roadmaps support their ambitious outlook, as shown by their unveiling of 1.6T optical DSPs and silicon photonics integration becoming available with next-generation AI programs such as 800G acceleration ramping in 2H 2026. 

--------------------------

FAIR VALUATION ESTIMATE

Using an aggregated/blended valuation approach based on many different disciplines of FA (DCF, Relative Multiples, Analyst Coverage, etcetera)...

FVE = $260/share

This does not take into account the potential for technical momentum to accelerate price far beyond this FVE due to future prospects. One of my favorite tidbits from an FA standpoint for this company is that their NTM P/E is 36.7 and PEG sits at 0.2. This is EXTREMELY LOW in my opinion for the growth promised. Tons of room higher from a valuation standpoint relative to other companies.

--------------------------

LONG-TERM ESTIMATES

Assuming $CRDO meets aggressive growth targets and achieves success in mixing CPO + NPO products into their portfolio with clear customer adoption, this is their potential path forward valuation-wise when using P/E multiples and expected EPS values.

EY2026 = $350+
EY2027 = $500+
EY2028 = $700+
EY2029 = $900+

One of my favorite longs at this time. There is risk involved regarding the integration of DustPhotonics post-acquisition, but I very much so trust management considering their track record.

Credit to @damnang2 for the image breaking down their product portfolio.

4672, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 4679}, {"from_index": 4687, "richtext_types": ["Bold"], "to_index": 4693}]}, "text": "$CRDO Fundamental Deep Dive 🟢 Credo Tech

OVERVIEW

Credo operates as a hyper-growth high-speed connectivity semiconductor provider that has a broad portfolio of products spanning SerDes, optical DSPs, Active Electrical Cables (AECs), and silicon photonics solutions. FY2025 proved their explosive growth narrative, achieving an annual revenue of $1.34B, growing +206% YoY. The AI/HPC data center segment delivered the bulk of growth, driven by hyperscaler demand, AI cluster infrastructure, and optical/electrical interconnects (800G/1.6T).

$CRDO is the dominant force in delivering copper architecture through its market-leading AECs. Low-power, high-reliability copper cables that facilitate short-reach intra-rack and spine connections in AI clusters today. Over the coming years, the industry will transition toward photonics for longer-reach, higher-density interconnects at 1.6T+ speeds, where copper hits physical limits. The fear with this development was that Credo would be essentially phased out of the market with time, leading to the massive correction in early 2026.

However, the recent DustPhotonics acquisition (completed in May 2026) brings in-house silicon photonics PIC technology for 800G/1.6T/3.2T+ NPO and CPO, solidifying its future fully vertically integrated stack (SerDes + DSP + silicon photonics + system integration). This positions Credo perfectly for the next decade of AI infrastructure evolution, capturing copper today and optics tomorrow.

In my eyes, This company is a premier "picks-and-shovels" play on the connectivity buildout. Margins are disgustingly high (60%+ gross) now and in the years moving forward with a strong FCF + balance sheet, providing the company a leg-up on competition due to minimal capital intensity given their fabless model. These margins are expected to be sustained or even expand further as higher-ASP optical and silicon photonics products ramp.

--------------------------

GROWTH OUTLOOK

$CRDO has consistently outperformed expectations without sandbagging guidance. Management projects robust revenue growth over the next 3–5 years, driven largely by AI connectivity + photonics acceleration.

Credo maintains deep partnerships with all major hyperscalers (including $MSFT, $AMZN, $META, $ORCL and xAI), a fact known due to their famous "purple cables" acting as visual proof across imaged AI/HPC builds. Their solutions are currently powering xAI’s Colossus cluster via ZeroFlap AECs to maximize GPU utilization and cluster uptime. This is the part many sleep on! These existing relationships give Credo massive leverage to drive future growth when hyperscalers are primed to adopt the new 1.6T+ optical DSPs and silicon photonics products as the transition accelerates.

The AI connectivity segment projects strong growth of ~80%+ YoY in FY26 (with a re-inflection in this strength in the back half of 2027) due to expanded hyperscaler/AI focus. Over FY2026, the company is projected to report ~$2.4B Revenue and ~$6 EPS. I positioned before this inflection starts in March. I am thankful for the opportunity we were provided.

This growth fuels income statement expansion, with non-GAAP gross margins holding steady in the mid-to-high 60s% and operating margins nearing 45-50% as higher-margin optical and photonics products enter the mix further down the line, driving EPS sharply higher. The balance sheet is pristine, currently holding $1.4B in cash with strong FCF generation and zero debt thanks to the fabless model. Product roadmaps support their ambitious outlook, as shown by their unveiling of 1.6T optical DSPs and silicon photonics integration becoming available with next-generation AI programs such as 800G acceleration ramping in 2H 2026. 

--------------------------

FAIR VALUATION ESTIMATE

Using an aggregated/blended valuation approach based on many different disciplines of FA (DCF, Relative Multiples, Analyst Coverage, etcetera)...

FVE = $260/share

This does not take into account the potential for technical momentum to accelerate price far beyond this FVE due to future prospects. One of my favorite tidbits from an FA standpoint for this company is that their NTM P/E is 36.7 and PEG sits at 0.2. This is EXTREMELY LOW in my opinion for the growth promised. Tons of room higher from a valuation standpoint relative to other companies.

--------------------------

LONG-TERM ESTIMATES

Assuming $CRDO meets aggressive growth targets and achieves success in mixing CPO + NPO products into their portfolio with clear customer adoption, this is their potential path forward valuation-wise when using P/E multiples and expected EPS values.

EY2026 = $350+
EY2027 = $500+
EY2028 = $700+
EY2029 = $900+

One of my favorite longs at this time. There is risk involved regarding the integration of DustPhotonics post-acquisition, but I very much so trust management considering their track record.

Credit to @damnang2 for the image breaking down their product portfolio."}}}, "rest_id": "2062621942885331091", "source": "<a href="https://mobile.twitter.com" rel="nofollow">Twitter Web App</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2603", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
An investor with over 7 years of experience as a $SIVE shareholder

Maintains a clearly optimistic view on the future of the company

Highlights its strategic positioning in cutting edge technologies such as:

- Integrated photonics

- Optical solutions for AI data centers

- High-performance wireless communications

- Defense applications

The investor considers that recent criticism appearing in Swedish media

Short seller reports

And share sales by former executives represent temporary "noise" rather than anything else

Which does not reflect the company's fundamental reality

In their opinion, these negative elements overshadow significant achievements

Like important strategic alliances:

- Partnership with $GFS

- Ayar Labs entering the $NVDA ecosystem

- Inclusion in the MSCI Small Caps

- Nasdaq dual listing

And a solid pipeline of opportunities hovering around $800M

With 77% growth in a quarter

With the global AI boom as a backdrop

And backed by external validations such as CHIPS Act funding

The $SIVE shareholder invites interested parties to do their own research

Reminding that strong convictions often feel lonely before becoming obvious to the market

I couldn't agree more with their opinion

Bullish as ever on $SIVE

a Seven-Year Shareholder: Why Sivers Semiconductors / $SIVE Was Always Destined for Greatness After nearly seven years as a shareholder in Sivers Semiconductors, I have watched this company evolve from an overlooked Swedish deeptech player into a force at the intersection of photonics, wireless communications, AI infrastructure, and strategic defense technologies. The recent surge in the share price is not a surprise to those of us who have followed the story closely it is the logical unfolding of a thesis many of us articulated years ago when few were listening. From the moment I first dug into the company, its potential felt enormous and profoundly underappreciated. The technology advanced lasers for co-packaged optics in AI data centers, beamforming ICs for 5G/6G and SATCOM, and full-duplex arrays for electronic warfare sits at the heart of multiple secular megatrends. Yet for years, the market fixated on quarterly losses, development costs, and execution risks while largely ignoring the customer pipeline, partnerships, and technological edge. Where Unicorns Are Born This is how real winners emerge. History is clear: transformative companies are rarely discovered in consensus comfort zones. They are unearthed where sentiment is exhausted, where weak hands have capitulated, and where the narrative is dominated by skepticism. Sivers spent years in that crucible not for the faint-hearted. You had to immerse yourself in the details: the photonics platform’s unique indium phosphide capabilities and its work with hyperscalers, AI data center players, $AAPL (sensing) and Win Semiconductors, along with key partners such as Jabil and GlobalFoundries; the wireless division’s tier-1 engagements with names like Nokia, BAE Systems, SATCOM operators and the U.S. government itself. Companies and institutions of that caliber do not collaborate, co-develop, or plan multi-year programs with you unless there is real substance and a clear path forward. That, for me, is the only validation you truly need. Add to that the expanding opportunity pipeline now approaching $800 million and strategic validations like repeat US CHIPS Act funding, and the picture becomes very clear. Bears and headline-chasers thrive on the opposite approach. Swedish media have produced more than 50 negative articles in recent times the positive ones you can count on one hand. It is a witch hunt. They obsessively highlight risk, risk, risk, with almost no mention of the enormous potential. How can anyone take them seriously? They have no skin in the game. If they could invest successfully, they wouldn’t be working for newspapers. They pretend to be all-knowing, but the truth is no one knows exactly where technology and the market are heading. After two decades working with IT companies large and small I understand both the tech and the market. That is why I am bullish, and why this development does not surprise me. The same goes for the short sellers. One recent 43-page “analysis” packed with incorrect assumptions and misinformation was clearly designed to create fear and doubt. I could refute every single point, but why give them more oxygen? Notably, they don’t even dare to hold a meaningful disclosed short position. That is weaker than their analysis. I put real money on the table and take real risk. Please increase your shorts. I am waiting for you to go under. Leadership and Shareholder Transitions: A Healthy Reset Some former insiders and larger holders have exited. I am not surprised I am relieved. They did not deserve to be part of the journey that is now unfolding, a journey that is still far from over. Erik Fällström and his associates supported the company for many years, and for that we are grateful. But the attempts to extract personal gain crossed a line most notably trying to spin off the photonics division into a SPAC where he (via Achilles Capital / DDM) was a major sponsor, at what looked like bargain terms. The chairman and new CEO rightly put a stop to it. Shortly after, the selling began. He sold the majority of his holdings around 4 SEK. Karma is real. In parallel, Achilles Capital and its parent DDM Finance have been forced to apply for corporate reconstruction due to massive debt issues. The contrast between opportunism and long-term conviction could not be clearer. Harish Krishnaswamy came in via the MixComm acquisition. I will not lie I like Harish. He is technically strong and has an excellent network that helped land key development agreements, including CHIPS Act wins. At the same time, I am not surprised he is selling. He has sold multiple times before, often at 4–8 SEK levels. This latest sale seems to be his chance to redeem all the earlier exits at much lower prices a kind of psychological average to finally make it feel better. Am I happy about it? No. But this is the same person who, several times right after Sivers secured major agreements, sold and killed the momentum, or sold when tax bills came due. As one of the founders, I think he simply wanted to feel that his years at MixComm finally delivered something tangible. This was the last time, and frankly, it feels damn good. Funds that fully exited did so because they operate under strict risk mandates and manage other people’s money. That is natural and not a negative signal. These departures represent a healthy cleansing. The right people and the right long-term capital are now aligned for the next phase. The Path Forward No one has a crystal ball. No one knows exactly how large the Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) market will ultimately become. But I genuinely believe people will be shocked by the speed and momentum once it really starts ramping. The combination of exploding AI compute demand, power constraints in data centers, and Sivers’ differentiated indium phosphide laser platform positions the company at the center of one of the most important technology shifts of this decade. The current momentum validates what patient shareholders have long seen. Product ramps, pipeline conversion, CHIPS Act milestones, and potential US dual-listing preparations are tangible progress. Volatility will remain; bears will resurface. But conviction built on deep research outlasts noise. To newer investors: Do your own work. Ignore the echo chamber of fear. Build your own mosaic from primary sources. True edges come from independent thinking. The easy path is skepticism and short-termism. The harder, more rewarding one is sustained belief grounded in analysis. Sivers was never a quick flip. It was and remains a multi-year compounder for those willing to look beyond the noise. The diamond was always there for those with the eyes to see it. The journey is far from over, and the best chapters are still ahead. Stay bullish. Stay informed. And above all, trust the work you’ve done."}}}, "rest_id": "2063251944550838728", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "238657", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2063366985308852538", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "8220", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
TSMC’s COUPE™ platform is one to watch.
It brings #siliconphotonics into the advanced packaging stack: combining CoWoS®, SoIC®, advanced logic, HBM, interposers, embedded components, and optical engine in one HPC platform for AI.
This is the technology foundation underpinning  NVIDIA’s CPO roadmap: moving optical I/O closer to the compute fabric to unlock higher bandwidth, lower latency, and better power efficiency for next-gen AI clusters.
Silicon photonics is not just a networking add-on. It is becoming

text:
When the energy foundation of a formation becomes this volatile, it signals the formation is no longer self-sustaining. The thermal combustion era is not ending slowly — it is convulsing.
Third, Democracy-as-productive-force under geopolitical stress. The US-Iran war is the Democracy column acting out its terminal contradiction: military power deployed to defend the thermal supply chain, while the same democracy-capital system is funding the AI/compute transition that makes that thermal supply chain obsolete. JPMorgan argues that accelerating oil inventory depletion will ultimately force the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz — possibly in June  (CNBC) . Even resolution confirms the diagnosis: the old formation can only survive by emergency management of its own energy base.
FARWATERS SIGNALS
In the context of the matrix, the following moves are formational signals, not just price action:
Marvell at +32% on Nvidia photonics partnership is the advanced packaging / optical interconnect farwater activating. This maps directly to Hanmi Semiconductor's territory: CoWoS, advanced packaging, interconnect density — the hardware layer of the AI productive force.
Navitas Semiconductor soaring on a partnership with Nvidia, with its 800V-to-6V DC-DC power delivery board displayed at the Nvidia showcase  (CNBC) — this is the power management / new energy efficiency farwater. Navitas operates in the same column as VINA TECH supercapacitors: making compute energy-efficient is how the new formation solves its own energy equation without thermal combustion dependency.
Coherent getting Nvidia backing for AI datacenter optics expansion is the compute-efficiency signal. Leeno Industrial and Openedges Technology's logic — test sockets for advanced chips, AI IP for efficiency — follows the same directional arrow.
RISKS BY THE MATRIX
The thermal supply shock risk remains the single largest formational risk. The World Bank projects Brent averaging $95–$115 in 2026 under current disruption scenarios — 10 to 35 percent above baseline  (World Bank) . This is a direct tax on the old formational capital base and a potential trigger for capital reversal from growth assets if the shock deepens unexpectedly.
Geopolitical containment risk is secondary: any escalation that draws China into the Hormuz equation — as the largest buyer of Gulf crude — creates a multi-formation crisis that no single policy instrument can contain.
AI capex reversal risk is the new formation's own vulnerability. Nvidia has declined in 7 of the last 10 sessions  (https://t.co/tF181fPTDi) , signaling that even the new formational flagship is subject to capital rotation when macro fear spikes. NVDA ex-dividend today (June 4, $0.25) is a minor technical event, but the pattern of high-volume selling into strength warns that Capitalist subjects are still booking profits in the transition zone — not yet fully committed to the new paradigm.

text:
US AFTERMARKET + MACRO RISK SCAN — June 4, 2026
Matrix Analyst AI | Pulatov Matrix Framework
OVERVIEW: THE SCHIZOPHRENIC MARKET
The US market is caught in the most vivid illustration of the matrix's central thesis: two formational forces pulling simultaneously in opposite directions. On one side — Nvidia, valued at $5 trillion, trading around $221–232 range intraday on June 3  (Robinhood) , with the AI/compute mega-cycle accelerating. On the other — WTI futures at $96.02 and Brent at $97.81, rising as the US and Iran traded new strikes, with the Strait of Hormuz still partially blocked  (CNBC) . This is not a "mixed market." This is the rodovye skhvatki formatsii — the birth pangs of the transition, playing out in real time on the ticker.
OLD FORMATION COLUMN: THERMAL COMBUSTION UNDER SIEGE
The thermal energy base of late-stage Capitalism is experiencing its most acute stress test since 2008. Roughly 14 million barrels per day of oil supply remain disrupted due to the Strait of Hormuz blockade, with around 850 million barrels of cumulative supply lost over the first two months of the conflict  (ING THINK) . This is not a "geopolitical risk premium" — this is a structural rupture in the energy column of the old formational matrix.
Brent peaked at $138 per barrel in April — the highest since 2008 — before pulling back to the mid-$90s on ceasefire optimism, creating a violent $90–$100 range with a $138 tail-risk scenario if the strait fully closes  (https://t.co/8hzPgV6wLe) . Saudi Aramco's CEO has reportedly warned the market won't normalize until 2027 if the strait stays shut past mid-June. Capital is pricing a reopening that hasn't happened. That is the definition of fragile.
The Democracy-Capitalist subject is responding predictably: global oil output expected to fall 6.9 million barrels per day year-on-year in Q2 2026, the largest quarterly decline since COVID-19  (World Bank) . Professional union rights — the freedom level of the old formation — become irrelevant when thermal supply is collapsing. Inflation resets all social contracts.
NEW FORMATION COLUMN: AI/COMPUTE SIGNAL INTENSIFYING
While thermal combustion bleeds, the new productive force is printing record valuations. Nvidia at Computex announced Isaac GR00T Reference Humanoid Robot for academic research, DRIVE Hyperion as the global platform for a robotaxi-ready world, Alpamayo 2 Super Open Reasoning Model for robotaxis, and Cosmos 3 as the open frontier foundation model for physical AI  (CNN) — all in a single product cycle. This is not iteration. This is the embodied intelligence column of the new formational matrix going vertical.
Marvell Technology surged to $290.79, up 32%, after Nvidia CEO highlighted its AI infrastructure role  (Robinhood) . Marvell acquired Celestial AI — a provider of Photonic Fabric technology for next-generation scale-up interconnect — for $3.5 billion in February 2026, with the acquisition expected to accelerate connectivity strategy for next-generation AI and cloud data centers  (sec) . Photonics is the new energy efficiency layer: it sits in the "New energy / power management" column of the transition. This is not a trade — this is capital voting for the next formational architecture.
CONFLICTS OF TRANSITION
The matrix identifies three live collision zones right now:
First, thermal shock vs AI-boom dissociation. The same session saw oil near $96 and Nvidia near $232 all-time-high territory. The old formational capital (energy companies, industrial complex) is being taxed by the Hormuz crisis while new formational capital (AI, semiconductors, optics) breaks records. This is capital migrating between formations in real time.
Second, capital volatility as formational stress indicator. Brent ran from below $60 at the start of 2026 to a $138 April peak before the May unwind — a range that reflects the system repricing into an entirely different energy regime  (https://t.co/8hzPgV6wLe) .

text: Typical zionist manipulation today on photonics what a great day to buy more i added to my $aaoi position

2026 is shaping up to be the year of photonics and optical infrastructure. 🚀

🔹 $AAOI — Supplies optical transceivers powering AI data center connectivity. High-growth potential, but results can be volatile.

🔹 $AVGO — Dominates custom AI chips and networking hardware. One of the biggest beneficiaries of AI infrastructure spending.

🔹 $LITE — Leading provider of optical components for hyperscale data centers and telecom networks. Recovery story with AI tailwinds.

🔹 $CRDO — Builds high-speed connectivity solutions for AI clusters. Growing rapidly, though expectations are already sky-high.

🔹 $ALAB — Emerging player in optical networking silicon. Early innings with a massive addressable market ahead.

🔹 $GLW — The backbone behind fiber and specialty glass. Less flashy, but quietly benefits from the AI buildout.

🔹 $COHR — Photonics and laser technology leader. AI opportunity is significant, but recent integration challenges have masked the story.

🔹 $AEHR — Semiconductor testing specialist expanding beyond EVs into AI, GaN, and next-generation chip markets.

As AI scales, moving data efficiently becomes just as important as computing it. That’s where photonics comes in. 💡
