text:
$ANET Fundamental Deep Dive 🟢 Arista Networks

OVERVIEW

Arista Networks operates as a leading provider of networking solutions across AI/HPC infrastructure and routing environments. FY2025 earnings showed significant momentum, with annual revenue of $9B, growing +29% YoY and guiding strongly. Hyperscaler demand and Ethernet fabric expansion delivered the bulk of growth, supported by 400G/800G switches scaling into higher-speed AI clusters. This is only the start of this vertical and will drive growth for years. Excited to see how their 1.6T+ programs and continued AI ramps perform in hyperscaler data centers in 2H 2026 and beyond.

Arista provides the full stack, providing high-performance Ethernet switches with EOS software. This allows ease of virtualization. This company is at the forefront of converting silicon into deployable, rack-ready systems at volume for hyperscalers. They're the pure-play leader enforcing the 1.6T+ photonics wave. It's a significant reason for my comfort in investing in the company as a leveraged AI networking play.

The previous earnings calls confirmed 1.6T+ data center switches are now ready to order, with XPO liquid-cooled pluggable optic modules delivering up to 12.8 Tbps per module. This advancement delivers massive scale and efficiency for hyperscaler AI clusters, addressing power and density limits in next-gen fabrics. The campus and routing segments provide more stable contributions, though with focus shifting toward high-margin AI data center opportunities.

Profitability is set to explode in the years moving forward as AI products mix deeper into the portfolio. Non-GAAP operating margin reached new highs around 47%+ with strong EPS growth. FCF remains exceptional with minimal capital intensity. Competition is present with $NVDA as the behemoth expands into the space with Spectrum-X; however, demand is rampant and Arista is the reliable, proven leader that networking engineers love according to feedback.

------------------------------------

GROWTH OUTLOOK

$ANET sits at a $220 Billion valuation. Its valuation reflects strong growth expectations with a ~60x Trailing P/E and ~24x P/S Ratio, indicative of the stock sitting at a premium. In my eyes, it's entirely deserved and can easily expand considering it's a extremely high margin business. Leveraging their +25-30% sequential YoY growth, it present a unique opportunity as a pure-play "picks-and-shovels" company.

Arista Networks has consistently outperformed expectations. Management projects robust revenue growth over the next 3–5 years, driven largely by AI data center + Ethernet acceleration. Anticipate a smooth rise in valuation. Their TAM is significant and growing exponentially. It's an extremely comfortable long moving forward. Product roadmaps support this outlook, as shown by their unveiling of 1.6T+ systems and XPO liquid-cooled modules set to hit the the income statement 2H 2026 onwards.

Over FY2026, the company is projected to report ~$11.5B Revenue and $3.60 adjusted EPS annually. This will be followed by even stronger growth in 2027, implying visibility and penetration improvement.

Risks include cyclical demand, supply chain execution, customer concentration, and broader macro slowdowns in tech spending.

------------------------------------

FAIR VALUATION ESTIMATE

Using an aggregated/blended valuation approach based on many different disciplines of FA (DCF, Relative Multiples, Analysts, etc.),

Current FVE = $240/share

This does not take into account its future prospects and potential for technical momentum to accelerate price far beyond this FVE.

------------------------------------

LONG-TERM ESTIMATES

Assuming $ANET consistently meets aggressive growth targets (revenue ramp achieved with margins maintained), this is their potential path forward valuation-wise when using P/E multiples and expected EPS values.

EY2026 = $300+
EY2027 = $450+
EY2028 = $600+
EY2029 = $800+

All in all, another high conviction investment that can multiply its valuation safely and securely over the next half a decade.

"$ANET Fundamental Deep Dive 🟢 Arista Networks

OVERVIEW

Arista Networks operates as a leading provider of networking solutions across AI/HPC infrastructure and routing environments. FY2025 earnings showed significant momentum, with annual revenue of $9B, growing +29% YoY and guiding strongly. Hyperscaler demand and Ethernet fabric expansion delivered the bulk of growth, supported by 400G/800G switches scaling into higher-speed AI clusters. This is only the start of this vertical and will drive growth for years. Excited to see how their 1.6T+ programs and continued AI ramps perform in hyperscaler data centers in 2H 2026 and beyond.

Arista provides the full stack, providing high-performance Ethernet switches with EOS software. This allows ease of virtualization. This company is at the forefront of converting silicon into deployable, rack-ready systems at volume for hyperscalers. They're the pure-play leader enforcing the 1.6T+ photonics wave. It's a significant reason for my comfort in investing in the company as a leveraged AI networking play.

The previous earnings calls confirmed 1.6T+ data center switches are now ready to order, with XPO liquid-cooled pluggable optic modules delivering up to 12.8 Tbps per module. This advancement delivers massive scale and efficiency for hyperscaler AI clusters, addressing power and density limits in next-gen fabrics. The campus and routing segments provide more stable contributions, though with focus shifting toward high-margin AI data center opportunities.

Profitability is set to explode in the years moving forward as AI products mix deeper into the portfolio. Non-GAAP operating margin reached new highs around 47%+ with strong EPS growth. FCF remains exceptional with minimal capital intensity. Competition is present with $NVDA as the behemoth expands into the space with Spectrum-X; however, demand is rampant and Arista is the reliable, proven leader that networking engineers love according to feedback.

------------------------------------

GROWTH OUTLOOK

$ANET sits at a $220 Billion valuation. Its valuation reflects strong growth expectations with a ~60x Trailing P/E and ~24x P/S Ratio, indicative of the stock sitting at a premium. In my eyes, it's entirely deserved and can easily expand considering it's a extremely high margin business. Leveraging their +25-30% sequential YoY growth, it present a unique opportunity as a pure-play "picks-and-shovels" company.

Arista Networks has consistently outperformed expectations. Management projects robust revenue growth over the next 3–5 years, driven largely by AI data center + Ethernet acceleration. Anticipate a smooth rise in valuation. Their TAM is significant and growing exponentially. It's an extremely comfortable long moving forward. Product roadmaps support this outlook, as shown by their unveiling of 1.6T+ systems and XPO liquid-cooled modules set to hit the the income statement 2H 2026 onwards.

Over FY2026, the company is projected to report ~$11.5B Revenue and $3.60 adjusted EPS annually. This will be followed by even stronger growth in 2027, implying visibility and penetration improvement.

Risks include cyclical demand, supply chain execution, customer concentration, and broader macro slowdowns in tech spending.

------------------------------------

FAIR VALUATION ESTIMATE

Using an aggregated/blended valuation approach based on many different disciplines of FA (DCF, Relative Multiples, Analysts, etc.),

Current FVE = $240/share

This does not take into account its future prospects and potential for technical momentum to accelerate price far beyond this FVE.

------------------------------------

LONG-TERM ESTIMATES

Assuming $ANET consistently meets aggressive growth targets (revenue ramp achieved with margins maintained), this is their potential path forward valuation-wise when using P/E multiples and expected EPS values.

EY2026 = $300+
EY2027 = $450+
EY2028 = $600+
EY2029 = $800+

All in all, another high conviction investment that can multiply its valuation safely and securely over the next half a decade."}}}, "rest_id": "2062265269427552310", "source": "<a href="https://mobile.twitter.com" rel="nofollow">Twitter Web App</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2016", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
I did say $AAOI was my favorite US optical long...

+20.1% today. 

If you want the next $SNDK, you're looking at it. 

I think H1 entering H2 2027 will likely be that massive inflection point for photonics players. 

We're just a tad early entering H2 2026 while everyone is building up capacity. 

Just a general rule of thumb in general if your name isnt space or quantum, markets are forward looking round 8 months. 

That revenue ramp inflection point is coming, more of a matter of when, waiting for it, and embracing volatility in the meantime.

text:
Portfolio changed since the last public list.

Main changes: added TEAM/NFLX starters, trimmed IREN, removed NOWL/VIAV/SOXS, and put a tiny SOXL sleeve back on. Also added some NBIS/NVDA/META/HLIT/CEG/ETHA exposure. Translation: still risk-on research, but less "spray and pray semis" than it looks.

My core holdings:

Fintech / software / AI apps — $HOOD, NOW, CRWD, RDDT, PLTR, LMND, TEAM, CRM, NFLX, SG

AI datacenter / compute — NBIS, IREN, NVDA, AMZN, META, HIVE, SANM, OSS

Memory / semis — MU, DRAM, MRAM, TSM, PDFS, CAMT, ONTO, AOSL

Photonics / networking — COHR, LITE, AAOI, GLW, HLIT

AI power — FLNC, CEG, VST, NVTS, ENPH

Crypto / beta sleeves — IBIT, ETHA, XRP, SOXL

Most of my research here is around photonics, memory, AI infra bottlenecks, and power. But the actual portfolio is broader: fintech, software, cyber, consumer internet, and a few tactical sleeves.

The house rule right now is boring on purpose: hold the real proof winners, don't add broad beta just because something got nuked, and only press when the proof or price actually improves.

Think of it like adding more appliances to your house without rewiring anything. You can do it up to a point. Rubin Ultra in 2027 hits that point. 576 GPU dies per rack pulling 600 kilowatts is physically impossible to run on the old wiring. So NVIDIA splits the rack into two separate units. One rack holds all the GPUs. A dedicated sidecar rack next to it handles all the power conversion at 800 volts DC. In the old system a 5kW power supply unit cost around $95 of semiconductor content. In the new 800V sidecar each unit is rated at 30kW and costs around $880 of semiconductor content. The per-unit cost goes up nearly 10x because the chips required to handle 800V cannot be made from regular silicon. You need silicon carbide and gallium nitride, which are more advanced and more expensive materials. Multiply that across an entire rack and you go from $20,186 of power semiconductors per rack at Rubin to $123,092 at Rubin Ultra. That gap does not go to $NVDA. It does not go to the companies building the racks. It goes entirely to the chip companies supplying the power semiconductors. text:
TSMC fumbles Copackaged optics for the Nth time like some fucking donkeys and now the whole industry is limping towards NPO, and the pod bros who price the entire AI TAM off Nvidia’s BOM line items still can’t actually explain what the problem is. So let me do the engineering for you, since clearly nobody on here will.

The bottleneck was never can you make light go through a waveguide. It’s all fucking thermals which is downstream of packaging. Specifically, how do you get a photonic engine onto the same substrate as a switch ASIC or XPU without your yield falling off a cliff and your reliability failing.

TSMC’s answer is CoWoS where they bolt everything onto one big monolithic silicon interposer. Cute, until you hit the reticle limit and start duct-taping interposers together (CoWoS-S, then -R, then -L, soon -PleaseStop). Every chiplet and HBM stack you add to that single interposer compounds your defect probability and one bad die leads to a five-figure package going into the dumpster. CoWoS is thermally retarded and the whole industry knows this and it’s why capacity “can’t expand” and Jensen is acting like a bouncer in the front of a club choosing who gets pass the velvet rope. 

There is ONLY one company that will make copackaged optics work and expand in the rack… it’s not Lumentum, it’s not Coherent, it’s fucking INTELLLL.

Intel’s EMIB gets rid of a giant reticle limited interposer and replaces it with a tiny silicon bridge that does the high-density coupling locally, exactly where you need it. You localize the hard part and the thermals in one area and your yield is ridiculously high. Comparing EMIB & CoWoS is so funny cause EMIB is north of 95% yield with like 12 reticle size equivalent package while CoWoS falls off a cliff after 5.5 reticles it’s that bad…now imagine adding thermally sensitive photonics.

People don’t know this but Intel has been doing silicon photonics in-house for ~25 years... In 2024 they showed an Optical I/O chiplet doing 2 Tbps bidirectionally at ~5 pJ/bit, with the PIC and EIC co-packaged right against the ASIC and it’s all because of EMIB. And even more critically than that, they’ve actually run the fiber-attach and reliability/test flow to JEDEC-grade standards already, which everyone hand-waves until their links flap in production.

My prediction is clear: Intel will capture over 90% of the copackaged silicon photonics market in the next five years because there is NO ALTERNATIVE.

So let me do the engineering for https://t.co/13LRsKoKfV", "id_str": "2063735227382677573", "is_quote_status": false, "lang": "en", "possibly_sensitive": false, "possibly_sensitive_editable": true, "quote_count": 24, "reply_count": 62, "retweet_count": 158, "retweeted": false, "user_id_str": "878311861384613888"}, "note_tweet": {"is_expandable": true, "note_tweet_results": {"result": {"entity_set": {"hashtags": [], "symbols": [], "urls": [], "user_mentions": []}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNjM3MzUyMjcxOTgxODU0NzI=", "text": "TSMC fumbles Copackaged optics for the Nth time like some fucking donkeys and now the whole industry is limping towards NPO, and the pod bros who price the entire AI TAM off Nvidia’s BOM line items still can’t actually explain what the problem is. So let me do the engineering for you, since clearly nobody on here will. The bottleneck was never can you make light go through a waveguide. It’s all fucking thermals which is downstream of packaging. Specifically, how do you get a photonic engine onto the same substrate as a switch ASIC or XPU without your yield falling off a cliff and your reliability failing. TSMC’s answer is CoWoS where they bolt everything onto one big monolithic silicon interposer. Cute, until you hit the reticle limit and start duct-taping interposers together (CoWoS-S, then -R, then -L, soon -PleaseStop). Every chiplet and HBM stack you add to that single interposer compounds your defect probability and one bad die leads to a five-figure package going into the dumpster. CoWoS is thermally retarded and the whole industry knows this and it’s why capacity “can’t expand” and Jensen is acting like a bouncer in the front of a club choosing who gets pass the velvet rope. There is ONLY one company that will make copackaged optics work and expand in the rack… it’s not Lumentum, it’s not Coherent, it’s fucking INTELLLL. Intel’s EMIB gets rid of a giant reticle limited interposer and replaces it with a tiny silicon bridge that does the high-density coupling locally, exactly where you need it. You localize the hard part and the thermals in one area and your yield is ridiculously high. Comparing EMIB & CoWoS is so funny cause EMIB is north of 95% yield with like 12 reticle size equivalent package while CoWoS falls off a cliff after 5.5 reticles it’s that bad…now imagine adding thermally sensitive photonics. People don’t know this but Intel has been doing silicon photonics in-house for ~25 years... In 2024 they showed an Optical I/O chiplet doing 2 Tbps bidirectionally at ~5 pJ/bit, with the PIC and EIC co-packaged right against the ASIC and it’s all because of EMIB. And even more critically than that, they’ve actually run the fiber-attach and reliability/test flow to JEDEC-grade standards already, which everyone hand-waves until their links flap in production. My prediction is clear: Intel will capture over 90% of the copackaged silicon photonics market in the next five years because there is NO ALTERNATIVE."}}}, "rest_id": "2063735227382677573", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "277705", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
구리에서 빛으로, 반도체의 한계를 넘는 여정
오늘 대화에서는 고성능 반도체 패키징 기술, 특히 HBM(High Bandwidth Memory)의 현재와 미래에 대해 깊이 있는 이야기를 나누었습니다.
현재 고속 연산을 담당하는 HBM은 D램을 여러 층 쌓아 올리고, 'TSV(Through-Silicon Via)'라는 미세한 구리 통로를 통해 데이터를 수직으로 전송합니다. 이것은 나노미터 단위의 초정밀 공정이 만들어낸 기적 같은 기술이지만, 데이터 전송량이 늘어날수록 전력 소모와 발열이라는 물리적 한계에 부딪히고 있습니다.
과거 우리가 지구촌 인터넷망을 연결할 때 전송 효율을 위해 구리선에서 광섬유로 대전환했던 것처럼, 반도체 패키징의 세계도 결국 '전기'가 아닌 '빛'으로 데이터를 전송하는 광학 기술(CPO, Silicon Photonics)의 시대로 향할 것입니다.
하지만 현재 양산 단계에서는 '구리'를 이용한 전기적 연결이 여전히 가장 현실적이고 신뢰성 높은 최선의 방식입니다. 우리는 지금 구리의 시대가 저물고, 빛의 시대가 시작되는 그 기술적 전환점의 한복판에 서 있습니다. 전력과 열이라는 인류의 과제를 해결하기 위한 새로운 소재와 구조 혁신이 계속되고 있는 것입니다.
